author: deme1744
content:
I noticed in v0.3.4 (and also v0.3.3) an issue which has nothing to do with stateless / witness but with the BAL.

Considering you add test cases on top of BAL branch I assume it has nothing to do with anything you added but rather the fact that you added two test cases that trigger this and don't exist under the BAL test cases.

Test cases involved are:
- `blockchain_tests/for_amsterdam/amsterdam/eip8025_optional_proofs/witness_7702/witness_codes_delegated_eoa_insufficient_balance.json`
- `blockchain_tests/for_amsterdam/amsterdam/eip8025_optional_proofs/witness_validation_codes/validation_codes_missing_delegated_code_on_insufficient_balance_call.json`

Our BAL implementation hits a "blockAccessListHash" mismatch. Which basically means the actual BALs differ.

I dug into it and it has to do with a call to delegated EOA that gets aborted due to insufficient balance. So the actual target with contract code to be run is never accessed, yet in the test vectors it does get added in the BAL. 

EIP7928 is not explicit about what should be done in this scenario (https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7928#eip-7702-delegation), but it seems off to add it.

For more details see: https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth1/pull/4210#issuecomment-4386440405

Anyhow, I realize this is likely not something that got added by zkevm but rather got triggered by added test cases. 
Nevertheless it would be good to get those test cases upstreamed to the bal eest tests. Not sure who is working on this hence why I drop it here.

author: jsign
content:
To ease looking into these, here are the links to the tests I created that triggered this for Nimbus:
- [test_validation_codes_missing_delegated_code_on_insufficient_balance_call](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/b1d870f8f06d9eba3d74179c39660cedb98f1f38/tests/amsterdam/eip8025_optional_proofs/test_witness_validation_codes.py#L286)
- [test_witness_codes_delegated_eoa_insufficient_balance](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/b1d870f8f06d9eba3d74179c39660cedb98f1f38/tests/amsterdam/eip8025_optional_proofs/test_witness_7702.py#L87)

I'll ping some people to double check if worth upstreaming these cases to `forks/amsterdam` and clarify the eip. thanks <@510413979080720385>
